revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Mônica | Guid=3005-4883 | Deck=Star of the Brazilian comic book series 'Turma da Mônica' (Mônica's Gang) by Maurício de Souza and star of various SEGA titles released under the label Tec Toy in Brazil. | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts=Concepts/Licensed_Game | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Monica_Bunny_Bashings,Games/Monica_e_a_Guarda_dos_Coelhos,Games/Monica_no_Castelo_do_Dragao,Games/Tap_Toy,Games/Turma_da_Monica_em_O_Resgate,Games/Turma_da_Monica_em_Vamos_Brincar_No_1,Games/Turma_da_Monica_na_Terra_dos_Monstros,Games/Wonder_Boy_in_Monster_Land | Locations= | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/2848/421950-monica31.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"421950-monica31.jpg","path":"0\/2848\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,square_mini,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> Star of the Brazilian comic book series 'Turma da Mônica' (Mônica's Gang) by Maurício de Souza and star of various SEGA titles released under the label Tec Toy in Brazil.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Morrigan | Guid=3005-11643 | Deck=Morrigan is a prominent figure in the Dragon Age universe, first introduced as a playable character in Dragon Age: Origins. Raised by Flemeth, the Witch of the Wilds, she is a powerful mage with devious tendencies and unknown motives. | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts=Concepts/Anti_Hero,Concepts/Atheism,Concepts/Bears,Concepts/Earth_Manipulation,Concepts/Exploring_Yourself,Concepts/Femme_Fatale,Concepts/Fire_Manipulation,Concepts/Healer,Concepts/Lightning_Manipulation,Concepts/Magic,Concepts/Narcissist,Concepts/Neutral,Concepts/On_The_Fly_Character_Switching,Concepts/Potential_Love_Interest,Concepts/Recruitable_Characters,Concepts/Revealing_Attire,Concepts/Ritual,Concepts/Sex,Concepts/Sexualized_Women,Concepts/Shape_Shifting,Concepts/Spellcaster,Concepts/Spiders,Concepts/The_Blight,Concepts/Tsundere,Concepts/Witch | Enemies=Characters/Alistair,Characters/Archdemon_Urthemiel,Characters/Flemeth,Characters/Leliana,Characters/Leske,Characters/Teyrn_Loghain | Franchises=Franchises/Dragon_Age | Friends=Characters/Dog_11655,Characters/Flemeth,Characters/Oghren,Characters/Shale,Characters/Sten,Characters/The_Warden,Characters/Wynne | Games=Games/Dragon_Age_Inquisition,Games/Dragon_Age_Origins,Games/Heroes_of_Dragon_Age | Locations=Locations/Ferelden,Locations/Haven_2832,Locations/Korcari_Wilds,Locations/Lothering,Locations/Redcliffe,Locations/The_Fade,Locations/Thedas | People=People/Claudia_Black,People/David_Gaider | Objects=Objects/Armor,Objects/Bed,Objects/Campfire,Objects/Crossbow,Objects/Dagger,Objects/Fingerless_Gloves,Objects/Gloves,Objects/Leather_Armor,Objects/Longsword,Objects/Magic_Staff,Objects/Robe,Objects/Short_Bow,Objects/Sword | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/9/99864/2669287-morrigan-001_5458.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2669287-morrigan-001_5458.jpg","path":"9\/99864\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_small,square_avatar,square_small,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":null},"background":{"file":"1713663-morrigan.jpg","path":"6\/60551\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_tiny,scale_super,screen_kubrick_wide,scale_large,scale_medium,scale_small,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,square_mini,screen_medium,square_avatar","caption":""}}' />
==Overview==
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/0/8169/1269622-morr2.png|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/8169/1269622-morr2.png|align=right|caption=The Witch of the Wilds}} A powerful mage from the [[Franchises/Dragon_Age|Dragon Age franchise]]. Though naive in many traditional ways of the world, Morrigan becomes one of the biggest power players in the in-universe's ever changing landscape.
'''Background'''
Morrigan was raised in the [[Locations/Korcari_Wilds|Korcari Wilds]] by [[Characters/Flemeth|Flemeth]], the 'Witch of the Wilds'. Having learned her magical craft outside the confines of the Circle of Magi, Morrigan is considered an Apostate, and has spent much of her life being hunted by Templars or avoiding persecution by the "Chasind" tribes of wild folk.
Because of the isolation caused by this lifestyle, Morrigan has relatively poor social skills, and tends to be heavily sarcastic in conversation and uncaring towards the needs of others. Morrigan is first encountered when the [[Characters/The_Warden|Warden]]-to-be is sent into the Wilds in preparation for the Joining. She leads them to Flemeth, who provides them with the treaties allowing the Grey Wardens to call upon various nations for aid during a Blight. After the Battle of [[Locations/Ostagar|Ostagar]], Flemeth orders Morrigan to accompany the Warden, supposedly for protection. While surprised, she reluctantly agrees, and sets off with the Warden and [[Characters/Alistair|Alistair]] to [[Locations/Lothering|Lothering]].
==Appearances==
===Dragon Age: Origins=== [[Games/Dragon_Age_Origins|''Main Article'']]
Although it quickly become apparent that Morrigan does not get along well with the other party members (such as continuously being on the opposite political end of magic compared to [[Characters/Wynne|Wynne]]) she is extremely useful in combat. As an Apostate, her attitude towards many of the Warden's interaction with other mages - and most people in general - often runs against what is usually considered culturally acceptable in [[Locations/Ferelden|Ferelden]]. Morrigan will frequently disapprove of more benevolent actions, although she is grateful when treated well in conversation, as she is used to hostile treatment by others. Morrigan does not hide the fact that she values power over many things and is not averse to using any means necessary to gain what she desires.
====Powers====
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/12/127694/1974823-1229879_morrigan2_1_.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/12/127694/1974823-1229879_morrigan2_1_.jpg|align=right|caption=Dragon Age: Origins CGI trailer - Casting a spell}} Morrigan is a powerful mage and shape-shifter and is able to assume the form of various animals. She starts off with the power to turn into a spider, but if developed further, she can assume the form of blight variants of the spider and bear as well as an insect swarm. Morrigan begins with a basic frost spell and other entropy spells, but can learn any other magic skills including healing spells. Morrigan can also teach the Warden and/or other party members how to shape-shift if they are a mage and have her approval metre maxed.
====Skills==== Morrigan is also skilled in herbalism and creating poison and claims to know 'at least fifteen types of poisons' that grow in the Korcari Wilds. While being asked by the Warden what other skills she has, Morrigan claims that she is also a good cook.
====Flemeth's Grimoire==== When attempting to free the [[Concepts/The_Circle_of_Magi|Circle of Magi]], Morrigan will mention to the Warden that Flemeth's Grimoire is located somewhere within the Tower. If it is found, Morrigan will eventually discover that while the book is not the true Grimoire, it does contain a great deal of information on Flemeth and her plans. Her extended life-span is not due directly to magic, but is made possible by her taking the body of a younger witch; Her 'daughters' are actually kidnapped at a young age and prepared as potential hosts. Morrigan would be the next in line. Shocked, Morrigan asks the Warden to kill Flemeth; refusing causes her to leave the party. When the Warden confronts Flemeth, the player is given the option of letting her go, and even of keeping the Grimoire instead of giving it to Morrigan, although this has no in-game benefits. Upon receiving the Grimoire, Morrigan states that it will be especially useful when confronting Flemeth again, as she is in all likelihood not actually dead.
====Romance====
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_medium/12/127694/1974822-1229879_morrigan2_1_.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/12/127694/1974822-1229879_morrigan2_1_.jpg|align=right|caption=Romance}} Morrigan is a romance option for male characters. While initially stating that the relationship is purely physical and that she has no intention of developing emotional attachment, the Warden can become deeply involved with Morrigan, to the point where she gives them a ring that tells her exactly where they are at all times. If Morrigan falls in love with the Warden, she will ask him to end the relationship, claiming that it will only end badly. The reason for this only becomes clear the night before the Final Battle.
====Morrigan's Ritual (End Game Spoilers)==== On the eve of the last battle against the Blight and the [[Characters/Archdemon_Urthemiel|Archdemon]], Morrigan will approach the Warden with an offer. Morrigan claims that if she is pregnant with a child, she can perform a ritual that takes the soul of the slain Archdemon and places it (uncorrupted as the soul of the former old-god) in the fetus; this would mean that whoever slays the Archdemon would survive, rather than be destroyed along with it. The child must be that of a Grey Warden to survive the ritual, and so either the Warden, Alistair, or even Loghain can be the father. If Morrigan has fallen in love with the Warden, she will beg him to agree, if only to save his life, and expresses regret that they must separate. If the Warden rejects her, she will leave the party immediately and not stay for the final battle. If she is impregnated, she will leave after the battle to raise the child on her own. It is not entirely clear what her true goal is in doing this, or even what the child will really be.
===Dragon Age: Origins - Witch Hunt=== The last DLC for DA: O is the final chronological addition to the storyline, taking place over 2 years after the main Origins story as well as after [[Games/Dragon_Age_Origins_Awakening|Dragon Age: Origins - Awakening]]. The Warden/Warden-Commander/Orleisian Warden-Commander returns to Flemeth and Morrigan's former home to attempt to track down Morrigan, who hasn't been seen since Origins' final battle, but has been reported to have returned to Ferelden.
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_medium/0/3661/1748863-2011_04_01_00009.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/3661/1748863-2011_04_01_00009.jpg|align=right|caption=Ready for departure}} The player will eventually find Morrigan activating an ancient Elvan magical mirror, an Eluvian (Elvish for seeing glass AKA a mirror), uncorrupted unlike the one found in the Dalish Elf origin. Morrigan permits a brief conversation before departing through the portal in which she claims to begin preparing for the upcoming changes in the Dragon Age universe (as started in Dragon Age 2 and evident in Dragon Age: Inquisition).
The two default interactions at the end of the DLC are to allow Morrigan to leave through the portal or to attempt to murder her. Only if the player has romanced Morrigan, stayed faithful to her and performed the ritual with her on the final night together, will Morrigan allow the player to reunite with her and depart through the portal to prepare for what the future holds together (and presumably meet their child).
===Dragon Age II=== [[Games/Dragon_Age_II|''Main Article'']]
Morrigan does not appear directly in this game, but is mentioned on various occasions by Flemeth in Sundermount, Alistair during the King Alistair quest and by [[Characters/Anders|Anders]] during dialogue about mages.
However, a picture of Morrigan appears very briefly on the final page of Cassandra's book in the epilogue.
===Dragon Age: Inquisition===
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/ignore_jpg_scale_small/0/2487/2499484-dragon_age_inquisition_morrigan_e3_2013.png|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/2487/2499484-dragon_age_inquisition_morrigan_e3_2013.png|align=right|caption=E3 2013 DAI Trailer - Morrigan in an Orlesian dress}} [[Games/Dragon_Age_Inquisition|''Main Article'']]
In the E3 2013 trailer Morrigan was revealed to be in the game (narrating about the state of affairs) and a possible focal point of the story, having already had prior knowledge, along with Flemeth and seemingly [[Characters/Sandal|Sandal]], of what calamities the Dragon Age universe would descend into.
It has been confirmed that the section of the Dragon Age: Origins ending which states that Morrigan would go on to infiltrate Orlais high society is canon, illustrated by the Orlesian dress worn by Morrigan.
==Other Info== * Morrigan is voiced by [[People/Claudia_Black|Claudia Black]].

revision.comment: Restore image alignment, sizes and captions from legacy source (3005-27444)
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Nico | Guid=3005-27444 | Deck=An NPC that appears in EarthBound. Unnamed in the Japanese version, she is named after the daughter of one of the game's localization writers. | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/EarthBound | Locations= | People=People/Marcus_Lindblom | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Nico is an NPC that that appears in [[Games/EarthBound|EarthBound]]. Found in Magicant, her only line is "La lalala. My name’s Nico… Let’s run and sing and dance!"
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_medium/0/6228/2524874-1483755958-nico..png|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/0/6228/2524874-1483755958-nico..png|align=right|caption=Nico's dialog in the original Japanese, in which she is nameless, and in the localized English.}} Nico does not have a name in the original Japanese version of EarthBound (Mother 2). She is named after the daughter of localization editor [[People/Marcus_Lindblom|Marcus Lindblom]], who was granted permission to insert her name in the game after she was born during the localization process and Lindblom subsequently had to work thirty straight days in order to meet localization deadlines.

revision.comment: Batch update: Fixed Image URL
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Olivia Benson | Guid=3005-58846 | Deck=Captain Olivia Margaret "Liv" Benson is the commanding officer of the Manhattan Special Victims Unit in New York City. Before taking over SVU, Benson worked as an SVU detective, and she was partnered with Detective Elliot Stabler for over thirteen years. | Concepts=Concepts/Character_Models_Based_on_Actual_People,Concepts/Detective,Concepts/New_York_City_Police_Department,Concepts/Voice_Acting | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Law_And_Order_Legacies | Locations=Locations/Manhattan,Locations/New_York_City | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/5/58746/2111314-screenshot1.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2111314-screenshot1.jpg","path":"5\/58746\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_tiny,square_avatar,scale_large,scale_small,scale_avatar,screen_kubrick,scale_tiny","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> Captain Olivia Margaret "Liv" Benson is the commanding officer of the Manhattan Special Victims Unit in New York City. Before taking over SVU, Benson worked as an SVU detective, and she was partnered with Detective Elliot Stabler for over thirteen years.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Orochi | Guid=3005-3252 | Deck=Orochi is an eight-headed, eight-tailed dragon from Japanese mythology. It typically appears in video games as some form of a boss, possibly most famously in Okami. | RealName=Yamata no Orochi | Gender=Male | Concepts=Concepts/Boss_Fight,Concepts/Demon_7650,Concepts/Divine_Punishment,Concepts/Japanese_Mythology,Concepts/Multi_Headed_Creatures,Concepts/Multi_Headed_Dragon | Enemies=Characters/Amaterasu,Characters/Issun,Characters/Kushinada_Hime,Characters/Nagi_11370,Characters/Sakuya,Characters/Shiranui,Characters/Susano,Characters/Waka | Franchises=Franchises/Devil_Survivor,Franchises/Digital_Devil_Saga,Franchises/Last_Bible,Franchises/Megami_Tensei,Franchises/Okami,Franchises/Persona,Franchises/Shin_Megami_Tensei,Franchises/SNK_vs_Capcom,Franchises/The_King_of_Fighters | Friends=Characters/Ajimi,Characters/Shizuku_Misawa,Characters/Spider_Queen | Games=Games/Counter_Side,Games/Dragon_Quest_1_2_3_Collection,Games/Dragon_Quest_Collection_25th_Anniversary,Games/Dragon_Warrior_III,Games/Gegege_no_Kitarou_Fukkatsu_Tenma_Daiou,Games/Megami_Tensei_Gaiden_Last_Bible_II,Games/Okami,Games/Puzzle_And_Dragons,Games/Puzzle_And_Dragons_Nintendo_Switch_Edition,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Devil_Survivor_2,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Digital_Devil_Saga,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Digital_Devil_Saga_2,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_NINE,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3_Portable,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_4,Games/Warriors_Orochi_4_Ultimate | Locations=Locations/Ark_of_Yamato,Locations/Japan,Locations/Moon_Cave,Locations/Shinshu_Field | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/1/13353/291619-orochi1.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"291619-orochi1.jpg","path":"1\/13353\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_small,square_tiny,square_small,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_medium,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":"Orochi"},"background":[]}' />
==History== Orochi, or ''Yamata no orochi'', (translated to "eight-forked-snake" in English) is a mythical beast from Japanese lore. It appears in two ancient texts, and in both versions he is slayed by Susanoo, god of sea and storm, after being expelled from heaven by Izanagi. Orochi is also always depicted as a being that requires sacrifices, and is slain before the eighth can reach him. According to the myths, it is killed by being made drunk and Susanoo recovers the sword Kusanagi from its tail, which is then presented to Amaterasu, his sister and Sun Goddess.
==In Games== As one can ascertain by reading the history of Orochi, the myth ties heavily into mainly two video games: [[Games/Okami|Okami]] and the [[Franchises/The_King_of_Fighters|King of Fighters]] franchise. The link to Okami is obvious, as the game was intended to be heavily based in Japanese mythology in the first place. Amaterasu and Susanoo both appear, and Orochi is slain in practically the same manner. The influence on King of Fighters, however, may not be so obvious to those who are not too big on the surprisingly complex lore of the franchise.
In what is known as the "Orochi Saga," taking place in KOF 95-97, [[Characters/Kyo_Kusanagi|Kyo Kusanagi]], [[Characters/Iori_Yagami|Iori Yagami]], and [[Characters/Chizuru_Kagura|Chizuru Kagura]]are the three fighters destined to take down the threat of Orochi. To make a very complicated story steeped in Japanese mythology short, Iori and Kyo's ancestors were in opposing clans in the past, while Chizuru's clan was neutral. They all ended up working together to take down Orochi, but Iori's clan made a pact with Orochi that strengthened them, but limited their lifespan. When Iori can no longer control his cursed blood, he becomes [[Characters/Orochi_Iori|Orochi Iori]], or "Riot Blood" Iori, a stronger and faster version of Iori with limited defense. In typical video game fashion, this is how they end up defeating Orochi (who was possessing the body of the New Face team's Chris' body), but nobody trusts Iori ever again.
For more on this version, see [[Characters/Orochi_39598|Orochi]].

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Orpheus | Guid=3005-4410 | Deck=Orpheus was a mortal musician in Greek mythology skilled in playing the harp and writing poetry. When his lover Eurydice died, he ventured into Hades to bring her back from the dead. | Gender=Male | Concepts=Concepts/Attachments,Concepts/Critical_Hit,Concepts/Fire,Concepts/Fusion,Concepts/Greek_Mythology,Concepts/Magic,Concepts/Mythology,Concepts/Persona,Concepts/Shuffle_Time,Concepts/Special_Attack,Concepts/The_Damned | Enemies=Characters/Death,Characters/Shuji_Ikutsuki | Franchises=Franchises/Megami_Tensei,Franchises/Persona,Franchises/Shin_Megami_Tensei | Friends=Characters/Aigis,Characters/Kotone_Shiomi,Characters/Makoto_Yuki,Characters/Messiah,Characters/Thanatos | Games=Games/Dont_Look_Back,Games/Hades_2020,Games/Monster_Strike,Games/Orpheus_in_the_Underworld,Games/Persona_5,Games/Persona_Q_Shadow_of_the_Labyrinth,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3_FES,Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3_Portable,Games/The_Battle_of_Olympus | Locations=Locations/Abyss_of_Time,Locations/Dorm,Locations/Tartarus,Locations/Velvet_Room | People= | Objects=Objects/Evoker,Objects/Harp,Objects/Scarf | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/1672/382027-orpheus_fullsize.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"382027-orpheus_fullsize.jpg","path":"0\/1672\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_small,square_tiny,square_small,square_mini,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_super,square_medium,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
==Overview== Orpheus is a figure in Greek mythology, the son of the Thracian king Oeagrus and the muse Calliope (some versions of the legend cast Orpheus' father as the god [[Characters/Apollo|Apollo]]). Apollo, fond of the young boy, gave him a small golden lyre, which Orpheus quickly mastered. Taught poetry and verse by his mother, Orpheus was so skilled at singing and making music that he was called "Master of Strings" and "Father of Songs".
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_small/1/17899/834582-orpheus.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/1/17899/834582-orpheus.jpg|align=right|caption=An oil painting of Orpheus and Eurydice by Jean-Baptiste-Camille Corot. Don't look back, idiot!}} Upon the death of his wife Eurydice, Orpheus was so distraught that his mournful singing brought even nymphs and gods to tears. Traveling to the underworld, he used his music to soften the hearts of [[Characters/Hades|Hades]] and [[Characters/Persephone|Persephone]], who allowed him to bring his wife back to the upper world, on the condition that he walk in front and not look back until they had both arrived on the surface. In his anxiety, Orpheus looked back, and saw his wife vanish, this time forever.
After Eurydice's second death, Orpheus became an apostate, refusing to worship the gods, save for Apollo, whom he thanked for his golden lyre. He also rejected the love of women, even that of the Thracian [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maenads Maenads], followers of the god [[Characters/Dionysus|Dionysus]]. Angered by his spurning, the Maenads threw sticks and rocks at him as he played, but his music was so beautiful that even the inanimate objects refused to strike him. Enraged, the Maenads tore Orpheus to pieces. His head floated down the Hebrus river, continuing to sing sad songs until it was found and buried on the island of Lesbos. His lyre was carried to the sky by the muses and placed among the stars.
==In Persona 3== Orpheus first appears in the game when the main character uses an [[Objects/Evoker|Evoker]] device to trigger his awakening, announcing himself with the line "Thou art I...and I am thou...from the sea of thy soul, I come forth...I am Orpheus...Master of Strings...". In the same scene, Orpheus briefly transforms into [[Characters/Thanatos|Thanatos]], another persona.
Orpheus' visual design is that of an android-like creature, with a mechanical body and a human head, whose face and hairstyle resemble that of the protagonist. Orpheus has a blue-white torso with a speaker in its center, a black face, white limbs, and wears a red scarf to cover his neck and bears a lyre. Some interpretations of the design suggest that it reflects Orpheus' legend, where after his death, only the head and lyre were left untouched, hence the completely mechanical body, yet human head and face. Orpheus uses his lyre as a melee weapon and strums it to cast magic.
===Gameplay and Trivia=== Orpheus belongs to The Fool arcana, the same arcana as that of the main character, and of the personae Decarabia, Slime, Legion, Susa-no-O, Black Frost, and Loki.
Orpheus initially bears the innate skill Agi (fire), and gains the skills Bash and Tarunda at levels 2 and 5. Orpheus prefers to inherit fire-based skills when fused. The fusion of Orpheus with the persona Apsaras of the Priestess arcana typically inherits the skill Cadenza.
With the [[Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3_FES|FES]] expansion (or the FES rerelease in north America), players could unlock a special version of Orpheus, known as Orpheus Telos (Japanese: Orpheus Kai), also of the Fool arcana. Orpheus Kai/Telos has the same design as the original Orpheus, except bearing a different color scheme. Orpheus Telos sports a red torso, pure white hair, face and eyes, and a light grey scarf. Interpretations of the design suggest that its changed white face and hair reflects the appearance of another key persona, [[Characters/Messiah|Messiah]], the ultimate incarnation of the Judgment arcana.
Orpheus Telos can only be obtained by maxing out all the possible Social Link relationships within one playthrough. Achieving this without the use of the [[Concepts/New_Game_Plus|New Game]] feature can be very difficult, requiring a delicate balance of attribute, relationship, and time management, while continuing to gain sufficient experience to climb Tartarus tower. Maxing out all the social links cues a special event in which [[Characters/Igor|Igor]] presents the protagonist with the Colorless Mask, a special item which enables the summoning of Orpheus Telos.
Orpheus Telos is unique in that it begins with no skills whatsoever (except the skill Victory Cry, obtainable with a level 97 fusion), and can inherit any possible skill. Carefully coordinated fusion and leveling of Orpheus Telos' component persona can result in an Orpheus Telos bearing many very powerful skills, ones sufficient even to defeat the immensely dangerous optional boss.
Orpheus Telos is described in the persona compendium with the line "''By bonding with many people, Orpheus was once again born from the sea of the soul. He has awakened to the power of Cipher, which holds endless possibilities."''
The word "Telos" is a Greek philosophical term for "end", "purpose" or "goal". This can be interpreted as Orpheus Telos representing the ultimate achievement, the one persona for whom there are infinite possibilities [of skill combinations], and is a reflection of the Fool arcana and the player himself.
===Persona 3 Portable=== In [[Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_3_Portable|Shin Megami Tensei: Persona 3 Portable]], the new playable [[Characters/Kotone_Shiomi|female main character]] also features Orpheus as her starting Persona. However, her version of Orpheus is female.
===Links to Other Shin Megami Tensei Games=== In [[Games/Shin_Megami_Tensei_Persona_4|Shin Megami Tensei: Persona 4]], the starting persona of the unnamed main character is Izanagi, a deity from Japanese mythology. Izanagi's role in Japanese legend is strikingly similar to that of Orpheus for the Greeks; Izanagi travels to the underworld, Yomi, to revive his deceased spouse, but ultimately fails. The similarity between the stories of Orpheus and Izanagi was almost certainly an attempt to forge a thematic link between the two starting personas, and by extension, the two main characters of Persona 3 and 4.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Pelé | Guid=3005-6313 | Aliases=Pele | Deck=Edison Arantes do Nascimento, better known as Pelé, was a former Brazilian soccer player rated by many as the greatest player of all time. He was given the title of Athlete of the Century by the International Olympic Committee and won 3 world cups as player. | RealName=Edison Arantes do Nascimento | Gender=Male | Birthday=1940-10-23 | Concepts=Concepts/CBF,Concepts/New_York_Cosmos_1970_1985,Concepts/Santos_FC,Concepts/Soccer_Player,Concepts/The_1970s | Enemies=Characters/Diego_Armando_Maradona | Franchises=Franchises/Captain_Tsubasa,Franchises/International_Superstar_Soccer,Franchises/Pro_Evolution_Soccer,Franchises/Winning_Eleven | Friends=Characters/Beckenbauer,Characters/Dunga,Characters/Jorginho,Characters/Kazu_Miura,Characters/Leonardo_Araujo,Characters/Manuel_Francisco_dos_Santos,Characters/Zico | Games=Games/Academy_of_Champions_Soccer,Games/Captain_Tsubasa_V_Hasha_no_Shougou_Campione,Games/FIFA_14,Games/FIFA_15,Games/FIFA_16,Games/FIFA_17,Games/FIFA_18,Games/FIFA_19,Games/FIFA_21,Games/FIFA_22,Games/Manchester_United_Championship_Soccer,Games/Pele,Games/Pele_II_World_Tournament_Soccer,Games/Peles_Soccer,Games/Pro_Evolution_Soccer_2008,Games/Pro_Evolution_Soccer_3,Games/Pro_Evolution_Soccer_4,Games/Super_Soccer,Games/Winning_Eleven_Pro_Evolution_Soccer_2007,Games/World_Soccer_Winning_Eleven_9 | Locations=Locations/Brazil | People= | Objects=Objects/Boots,Objects/Goal,Objects/World_Cup_Trophy | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/1/13535/545524-pele.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"545524-pele.jpg","path":"1\/13535\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_avatar,scale_small,square_small,square_tiny,square_mini,square_medium,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> He died on December 29 2022.
==Senior career== 1956–1974 [[Concepts/Santos_FC|Santos]]
1975–1977 [[Concepts/New_York_Cosmos_1970_1985|New York Cosmos]]
==National team== 1957–1971 [[Concepts/CBF|Brazil]]

{{Character | Name=Phenomaman | Guid=3005-58772 | Deck=A Superman-esque hero from the world of Dispatch. Though powerful, he struggles with human social cues due to being an alien from another world, and is easily depressed since his break-up with Blonde Blazer. | Concepts= | Enemies=Characters/Armstrong,Characters/Coupe,Characters/Shroud_58847 | Franchises= | Friends=Characters/Blonde_Blazer,Characters/Chase_58771,Characters/Coupe,Characters/Golem_58781,Characters/Invisigal,Characters/Prism,Characters/Robert_Robertson_III | Games=Games/Dispatch | Locations= | People= | Objects= | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/77/776653/3821245-dossier_phenomaman.png}}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3821245-dossier_phenomaman.png","path":"77\/776653\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_small,scale_super,square_medium,scale_medium,square_tiny,scale_small,square_mini,square_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_avatar,scale_large","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> A Superman-esque hero from the world of Dispatch. Though powerful, he struggles with human social cues due to being an alien from another world, and is easily depressed since his break-up with Blonde Blazer.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Phineas | Guid=3005-24177 | Deck=A mysterious wandering man, he loves spreading happiness and joy where ever he goes. | Gender=Male | Concepts=Concepts/amiibo | Enemies= | Franchises=Franchises/Animal_Crossing,Franchises/Nintendo_Clocks | Friends= | Games=Games/Animal_Crossing_City_Folk,Games/Animal_Crossing_Clock | Locations= | People= | Objects=Objects/Balloon | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/15/151939/2296161-animalcrossingcityfo_102308_14205.png }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2296161-animalcrossingcityfo_102308_14205.png","path":"15\/151939\/","mime":"image\/png","sizes":"square_avatar,square_small,scale_small,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_mini,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' />
==[http://www.giantbomb.com/animal-crossing-city-folk/3030-21096 Animal Crossing: City Folk]== In Animal Crossing: City Folk, Phineas would occasionally appear in the city and give away free toys such as balloons, pinwheels and bubble wands.
==[http://www.giantbomb.com/animal-crossing-city-folk/3030-21096 Animal Crossing: New Leaf]==
{{GBFigure|src=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_small/17/174341/2539972-4306319432-Phine.jpg|full=http://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/original/17/174341/2539972-4306319432-Phine.jpg|align=right|caption=Phineas's New Threads}} In Animal Crossing: New Leaf, Phineas is now a Scout Master and gives out badges when certain tasks are completed. He will appear in your town and will wander until you find him and ask him for a badge (politely, of course).

{{Character | Name=Phoxhunter | Guid=3005-58728 | Deck=The main protagonist of Duet Night Abyss, a castaway from Purgatorio Island. | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Duet_Night_Abyss | Locations= | People= | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' /> The Phoxhunter's name and gender are chosen by the player. Whichever body they don't choose will be watching over them in their dreams.

revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Ping | Guid=3005-25928 | Deck=Ping is a gynoid PlayStation 2 accessory that lives with Piro and Largo in the online comic "Megatokyo". | Gender=Non-Binary | Concepts=Concepts/Android | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Crisis_Heart_Brawlers_Clash_at_Otakon | Locations= | People=People/Cristina_Vee | Objects=Objects/PlayStation_2 | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/16/164924/2460544-ping.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"2460544-ping.jpg","path":"16\/164924\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"square_small,scale_small,square_mini,square_avatar,square_tiny,square_medium,scale_medium,screen_medium,scale_large,scale_avatar,scale_super,screen_kubrick","caption":""},"background":[]}' /> Ping makes an appearance in [[Games/Crisis_Heart_Brawlers_Clash_at_Otakon|Crisis Heart Brawlers: Clash at Otakon]], voiced by [[People/Cristina_Vee|Cristina Vee]].

{{Character |Guid=3005-58627 |Name=Pinky Dinky Doo |Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/239/2391650/3809804-pinkydinkydoo.jpg |Deck=The main character from the animated show of the same name. |Friends=Characters/Tyler Dinky Doo |Games=Games/Pinky_Dinky_Doo_Eat_It_or_Wear_It }} <div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"3809804-pinkydinkydoo.jpg","path":"239\/2391650\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_super,scale_medium,square_small,square_medium,square_tiny,scale_small,square_mini,square_avatar,screen_medium,screen_kubrick,scale_avatar,scale_large","caption":null},"background":[]}' /> The main character from the animated show of the same name.

{{Character | Name=Pitbull | Guid=3005-58567 | Deck=Large fighter from the Bronx who turned down a pro boxing career to join the Army. Now a heavy weapons specialist and squad leader with Delta Force. | RealName=Rydel Wilson | Concepts= | Enemies= | Franchises= | Friends= | Games=Games/Delta_Force_Land_Warrior | Locations= | People= | Objects= }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":[],"background":[]}' /> Large fighter from the Bronx who turned down a pro boxing career to join the Army. Now a heavy weapons specialist and squad leader with Delta Force.

revision.comment: Point cover at a rendition that actually serves
revision.text:
{{Character | Name=Pom Pom | Guid=3005-6457 | Deck=Pom Pom speaks with bubbly noises and is from the Isle of Pom. | Concepts=Concepts/Lack_of_Visible_Body_Parts,Concepts/Martial_Arts,Concepts/Mute_Character,Concepts/Race_to_the_End_of_the_Race | Enemies= | Franchises=Franchises/Homestar_Runner,Franchises/Sam_And_Max | Friends=Characters/Bubs,Characters/Coach_Z,Characters/Homestar_Runner,Characters/Marzipan,Characters/Strong_Bad,Characters/Strong_Mad,Characters/Strong_Sad,Characters/The_Cheat,Characters/The_Poopsmith | Games=Games/Dangeresque_The_Roomisode_Triungulate,Games/Poker_Night_at_the_Inventory,Games/Strong_Bads_Cool_Game_for_Attractive_People,Games/Strong_Bads_Cool_Game_for_Attractive_People_Episode_1_Homestar_Ruiner,Games/Strong_Bads_Cool_Game_for_Attractive_People_Episode_2_Strong_Badia_The_Free,Games/Strong_Bads_Cool_Game_for_Attractive_People_Episode_3_Baddest_of_the_Bands | Locations=Locations/Club_Technochocolate,Locations/Free_Country_USA | People=People/Matt_Chapman,People/Mike_Chapman | Objects=Objects/Basketball,Objects/Cell_Phone,Objects/PDA,Objects/Total_Load | Image=https://www.giantbomb.com/a/uploads/scale_super/0/4938/603548-sbcg4ap102_pompom.jpg }}
<div id='imageData' data-json='{"infobox":{"file":"603548-sbcg4ap102_pompom.jpg","path":"0\/4938\/","mime":"image\/jpeg","sizes":"scale_medium,scale_super,square_mini,square_medium,square_small,screen_medium,square_avatar,scale_small,scale_large,scale_avatar,square_tiny,screen_kubrick","caption":"Pom-Pom chills."},"background":[]}' /> He is considered to be the "coolest" resident of Free Country USA, and is often aloof. He has both a cell-phone and a Pom Pilot.
Pom Pom is also an accomplished martial artist.
If Strong Bad has his wish, Pom Pom would be colored like an ABA Basketball.

revision.comment: Batch update: Fixed Image URL
revision.text:

{{Character |Guid=7b5064d4-da01-43a3-9b3a-eb4b58966a5a |Name=Porkchop |Image=Porkchop.png |Deck=He's a little pet dog from Doug. |Gender=Male |Friends=Characters/Doug Funnie |Games=Games/Disneys Doug Dougs Big Game }} He's a little pet dog from Doug.
